This family includes a wide range of plants, many of which are edible, such as tomatoes and potatoes. intro: Solanum sabinei, also known as Sabine's nightshade, is a plant species in the Solanaceae family, characterized by its toxic and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Solanum sabinei include glycoalkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sabine's nightshade is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used topically as a poultice or salve to treat various health conditions.
